summary: Google revealed major updates for Android ahead of I/O 2026, including a new laptop category called Googlebook, Gemini intelligence for and a redesign of Android Auto.&nbsp;

title: Anthropic overtakes OpenAI in enterprise artificial intelligence race
summary: New data reveals Anthropic has surpassed OpenAI in enterprise AI spending, with 34.4% of surveyed companies paying for its products in April. This shift is largely driven by demand for Anthropic's AI coding assistant, Claude Code, marking a significant disruption in the market.

title: TSMC says global chip market to hit $1.5 trillion by 2030 as AI drives growth
summary: TSMC predicts the global chip market will surpass $1.5 trillion by 2030. AI and high-performance computing will lead this growth. The company is expanding its advanced chip production and packaging capacity. New facilities are being built in Arizona, Japan, and Germany to meet future demand.

title: Nvidia CEO hopes Trump and Xi will improve two-way ties: CCTV
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang met with Chinese President Xi Jinping. He expressed hope for improved US-China relations. Nvidia faces difficulties supplying advanced AI chips to Chinese clients. This impacts their artificial intelligence development efforts.

title: Samsung Electronics urges union to resume talks as strike threat looms
summary: Samsung's labour union has rejected a call to resume pay talks, demanding transparency in the company's bonus scheme. A threatened 18-day strike looms if demands aren't met, with the government urging both sides to negotiate to protect South Korea's vital chip export economy.

title: US chip start-up Cerebras to raise $5.5 billion in IPO
summary: Cerebras Systems is set to launch its IPO on Wall Street at $185 per share, aiming to raise approximately $5.5 billion and valuing the company at over $55 billion. This marks the largest US IPO this year, with the chip startup specializing in wafer-scale processors crucial for AI development.

title: Microsoft eyeing startup deals for life after OpenAI
summary: Microsoft is actively seeking AI startups for potential acquisitions. This move aims to bolster its AI talent and develop a cutting-edge AI model independently. The company is reportedly in discussions with Inception, a startup focused on novel large language model development.

title: Nvidia CEO's foundation buys $108 million of AI computing from CoreWeave, donates it to researchers
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang and his wife are donating $108.3 million in computing time from CoreWeave to universities for AI research. This significant gift also bolsters CoreWeave, an AI cloud company in which Nvidia has invested heavily and secured substantial cloud capacity deals.

title: US defence firm Anduril raises $5 billion, doubling its valuation to $61 billion
summary: Defence tech firm Anduril Industries has secured a massive $5 billion funding, catapulting its valuation to $61 billion. This surge follows a year of significant growth, with revenue doubling to $2.2 billion and workforce expansion. Investors are increasingly backing defence innovators amidst global

title: Ex-Peak XV trio raises Mettle; Fintechs go licence hunting
summary: Happy Thursday! Three former Peak XV MDs, who abruptly left the VC firm, are launching Mettle Capital to back Indian startups. This and more in today's ETtech Morning Dispatch.

title: Mphasis playing host to a high-stakes game of executive musical chairs
summary: Mphasis faces a wave of senior executive departures. Over a dozen high-performing leaders have left since 2025 for competitors such as Coforge and Tenarai.
